summary: Blob.FromStream in HarfBuzzSharp uses `fixed` to temporarily pin a managed byte array, but the `hb_blob_create` native call retains the pointer beyond the fixed block's scope. Once the GC moves the unpinned array, the blob's pointer becomes dangling, causing AccessViolationException or corrupted glyph data.

stepsToReproduce: Use the blob (e.g., create a Face and shape text)
If GC runs between blob creation and usage, the managed array backing the blob may be relocated
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: All platforms — GC behavior is runtime-dependent but the bug exists on all .NET runtimes

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The FromStream implementation has not changed since the method was introduced. The bug exists in all versions of HarfBuzzSharp.

fixStatus.reason: PR #3473 is open and addresses this exact issue by switching to Marshal.AllocCoTaskMem for unmanaged memory allocation. Not yet merged.

analysis.summary: The FromStream method copies stream data into a managed byte array via MemoryStream.ToArray(), then uses `fixed` to pin it only during the Blob constructor call. However, hb_blob_create with MemoryMode.ReadOnly retains the pointer for the lifetime of the blob. After the fixed block exits, the GC can relocate the array, making the pointer dangling. This is a well-known pattern bug confirmed by a user in #2323 who experienced AccessViolationException and zeroed GlyphInfos.
analysis.rationale: This is clearly a bug — the code passes a pointer to managed memory that can be moved by the GC after the fixed block exits. The TODO comment in the code ('check to see if we can avoid the second copy') acknowledges the implementation is suboptimal. The area is HarfBuzzSharp (not SkiaSharp core) since the Blob class is in the HarfBuzzSharp namespace. Severity is high because it causes intermittent crashes that are hard to diagnose.

keySignals.text: fixed (byte* dataPtr = data) { return new Blob((IntPtr)dataPtr, ...); }
keySignals.interpretation: The fixed block only pins the array during the constructor call. After it exits, the pointer is dangling.

keySignals.text: // TODO: check to see if we can avoid the second copy (the ToArray)
keySignals.interpretation: Existing TODO acknowledges the implementation needs work.

keySignals.text: Can confirm this is actually a problem. At best all codepoints in Buffer.GlyphInfos becomes 0, at worst AccessViolationException will be thrown.
keySignals.interpretation: Independent confirmation that this bug manifests in real-world usage with observable symptoms.

keySignals.text: hb_blob_create(void* data, uint length, MemoryMode mode, ...)
keySignals.interpretation: HarfBuzz stores the pointer — with ReadOnly mode it does not copy the data, it reads from the provided address.

codeInvestigation.finding: FromStream uses MemoryStream.ToArray() to get a managed byte[], then pins it with `fixed` only during the Blob constructor. The release delegate `() => ms.Dispose()` disposes the MemoryStream but does not prevent the array from being moved by GC.
The Create method passes the pointer to hb_blob_create with MemoryMode.ReadOnly. HarfBuzz retains this pointer for the blob's lifetime without copying the data.
SKData.CreateCopy uses fixed correctly because sk_data_new_with_copy copies the data immediately during the fixed block. The Blob case is different — hb_blob_create retains the pointer.
SKImage.Create uses Marshal.AllocCoTaskMem for unmanaged memory allocation — the same pattern that PR #3473 applies to fix Blob.FromStream.

workarounds: Allocate unmanaged memory with Marshal.AllocHGlobal, copy stream data into it, and create the Blob with the unmanaged pointer and a release delegate that calls Marshal.FreeHGlobal

resolution.hypothesis: The managed byte array from MemoryStream.ToArray() is only pinned during the Blob constructor (fixed block), but hb_blob_create retains the pointer. After the fixed block exits, GC can move the array, leaving a dangling pointer.

proposals.title: Merge PR #3473
proposals.description: PR #3473 replaces the managed array with Marshal.AllocCoTaskMem unmanaged allocation. It also handles non-seekable streams (buffers into MemoryStream first) and empty streams (returns Blob.Empty). This is the correct fix.

proposals.title: Manual unmanaged allocation workaround
proposals.description: Users can work around the issue by manually allocating unmanaged memory instead of using Blob.FromStream.

resolution.recommendedReason: PR #3473 already provides a clean, tested fix with proper handling of edge cases (non-seekable streams, empty streams).

actionability.reason: Confirmed bug with an open PR (#3473) ready for review. The fix is straightforward — review and merge the PR.

actions.description: Fix area label from area/SkiaSharp to area/HarfBuzzSharp
Cross-reference with older duplicate #2323 reporting the same issue
Cross-reference with broader GC safety issue #3394
Acknowledge the bug, confirm it's a known issue, point to the existing PR
